reasoning: The informal statement uses Pugh's standard definition: a point p condenses E iff every neighborhood of p contains uncountably many points of E. The Lean statement asserts ∃ p, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E), which says every set in the neighborhood filter of p has uncountable intersection with E — exactly the condensation-point condition. Mathlib's 𝓝 p is the filter of sets containing an open nbhd of p, which is equivalent to "every open nbhd of p has uncountable intersection with E" (the stronger condition for all filter members follows from the open case, and vice versa). The hypothesis `¬ Set.Countable E` correctly captures "uncountable subset of ℝ", and the ambient type ℝ matches. No extra hypotheses are added and no quantifier structure is altered.
